title: carthamodes coeruleum
intro: Carthamodes coeruleum, also known as blue wormwood, is a plant species that belongs to the daisy family. It is primarily used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat a variety of health issues, such as fever, digestive problems, and respiratory infections, due to its health benefits, including its ability to reduce inflammation and fight off infections. In herbal preparations, Carthamodes coeruleum is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
